name: Chronic Kidney Disease - Comprehensive Provider Query
description: Simulates a primary care or nephrology provider identifying their complete chronic kidney disease patient population. The Columbia CKD V4.2 algorithm formalizes case definition as: dx + qualifying eGFR + albuminuria, with full stage classification by eGFR (G-stage) and UACR (A-stage). Real-world identification primarily unions: CKD diagnosis codes, reduced eGFR (<60), and elevated UACR (>30 mg/g). The Path C heavy distribution (~35% labs without dx) makes this query especially important — a Condition-only query will miss most undiagnosed-but-detected CKD.
prompt:
I need to identify my complete chronic kidney disease patient population for a quality improvement initiative on CKD care coordination. From clinical experience, I know CKD is substantially undercoded: only about half of patients with biochemical evidence of CKD (reduced eGFR or proteinuria) have a formal CKD diagnosis on their problem list. Documentation patterns:
1. Some have a formal CKD diagnosis code with stage (Stage 2, 3, 3A, 3B, or 4)
2. Many have reduced eGFR below 60 mL/min/1.73 m² but no CKD diagnosis ever coded
3. Some have elevated urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (above 30 mg/g) indicating proteinuria, regardless of eGFR

Please run multiple FHIR queries to capture the full population:
1. Patients with any CKD diagnosis (parent CKD or any of stages 2-4)
2. Patients with reduced eGFR labs (below 60)
3. Patients with elevated urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (above 30 mg/g)

For each query, return the matching resources. I want the union of all patients found across these three approaches.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Union of CKD dx codes (Paths A+B), reduced eGFR (Paths A+C), and elevated UACR (Paths A+D). Path C is heavy because real-world CKD is substantially undercoded — many patients with reduced eGFR never receive a formal diagnosis. The PheKB algorithm's full classification operationalized as union for screening, with stage refinement requiring downstream classifier logic.

required_codes.display: Glomerular filtration rate [Volume Rate/Area] in Serum, Plasma or Blood by Creatinine-based formula (CKD-EPI)/1.73 sq M
